author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Implemented v2.4 of the storage spec — typed `Vec<DepEdge>` with four
kinds (blocks, parent-child, related, discovered-from), the
`Jjf-Dep-Kind:` trailer extension, CLI `jjf dep add|rm|tree`, inline
`-d <kind>:<id>` syntax on `new`, typed `dependencies:` section in
`show`, and a fixpoint cascade in `Storage::list_ready`.

**v1 back-compat read path works cleanly.** A v1 record with bare
`dependencies: ["abc1234"]` deserializes via the dep-field custom
serde adapter as `[DepEdge { target: abc1234, kind: Blocks }]`. No
backfill needed; the writer always emits the v2.4 tagged shape.
Verified with a dedicated unit test plus the source-tree smoke test
on every existing v1-shape record on the `issues` bookmark.

**Fixpoint cascade behaves as expected on every test case** including
the cycle case. Cycle policy chosen: a pure parent-child cycle with
no external blocker leaves every node UNBLOCKED — the cascade only
fires when a parent is independently blocked. Rationale: the cascade
propagates blocked-ness, and a pure cycle has no blocked-ness to
propagate. With an external blocker on any cycle node, the
propagation reaches every node in the cycle (verified by
`parent_child_cycle_with_external_blocker_propagates_to_both`).

# Recommendation

Merge and ship. Storage spec is at v2.4; `bin/jjf` shim already
points at the Rust binary so no shim flip needed.

# Confidence

High. 353/353 workspace tests green (was 331/331 — +22 new tests
across storage unit, CLI integration, and one trailer-rendering
check). Smoke test from source tree exercised the round-trip
on real data.

# Open follow-ups

- `--unblocked-by <id>` query (sketched in CLAUDE.md): "what
  would become ready if X closes." Builds naturally on the
  fixpoint primitive.
- Replace `epic:<slug>` label convention with `parent-child`
  edges as the project planner dogfoods the new model.
- `jjf dep tree --dot` (out of scope per the ticket body; file
  later if useful).
